SOUNDNESS_CORRECTNESS.justification: The paper articulates plausible design patterns and provides empirical comparisons (Section 4.2) using FractalNet as a baseline, but many claims are supported only by limited CIFAR-10/100 experiments without statistical tests or ablations. Mathematical arguments (e.g., Appendix A on equivalence of concatenation/summation under weight-sharing) are high-level and informal rather than rigorous, and several proposed architectures (FoF, SBN, TSN) are evaluated only in a narrow set of trials with mixed results (Section 4.2). There are also unsupported generalizations (e.g., broad applicability of the 14 patterns) beyond the scope of the experiments, so conclusions are partly justified but not fully validated.

ORIGINALITY.justification: Framing CNN architecture choices as a set of 'design patterns' is a novel organizational perspective compared with the usual papers proposing a single architecture, and the paper proposes several concrete variants (Fractal of FractalNet, freeze-drop-path/SBN, Taylor Series Networks) described in Section 4.1 and Appendix B. However, many ideas build directly on existing work (e.g., residuals, drop-path, fractal modules, polynomial/multiplicative interactions) and the new architectures are incremental adaptations rather than fundamentally new paradigms. The contribution is therefore a moderately original synthesis plus incremental architectural tweaks, not a radical departure from prior work.

CLARITY.justification: The paper is generally well structured — it lists the 14 design patterns clearly (Section 3), describes architectural innovations (Section 4.1), and provides implementation details in Appendix B — which makes the main ideas easy to find. Nonetheless, the manuscript contains repetitions and some presentation issues (duplicated paragraphs, informal explanations in Appendix A), and several experimental details (exact hyperparameter sweeps, statistical variability) are only partially specified, reducing reproducibility and readability in places.
